MODULE ONE – The Sideways Sales Letter™

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Executive Summary:
• 	The Sideways Sales Letter is the core strategy to all of PLF. Now keep in mind, there are
many different paths to success. But over 10,000 students (including myself) have arrived
at success by using this core strategy.
• 	The Sideways Sales Letter is the opposite of traditional old-school sales letters. We’re
basically taking the long form sales letter, turning it sideways, and making it a 10 day
sequence.
• 	This is very different from sending one sales message that sends people to your sales
page/letter and hoping they buy (AKA hope marketing). The sideways sales letter is what
converts now and is a more modern style.
• 	People’s “B.S. detectors” turn on and become highly skeptical when they see a long-form
sales letter. With the Sideways Sales Letter, you move people from skepticism to TRUST.
• 	Initially, you’ll send out 3 pieces of pre-launch content (PLC). This allows you to share
valuable content with your prospects and spread it out over time. But more importantly,
you build rapport with your prospects and customers.
• 	PLC video #1: The opportunity. Show prospect that they can make a change and get
results.
• 	PLC video #2: The transformation. More teaching and content. You’ll gain
likeability at this stage and begin building community
• 	PLC video #3: The ownership experience. What will it feel like to own your
product?
• 	When you publish good content for free you are instantly seen as an authority. By giving
content away for free to people they will feel a need to give back through the law of
reciprocity.
• 	You’ll get social proof by offering the free content, and people will see the comments that
are left by viewers. During the release of the PLC videos you’ll build massive amounts of
trust and people will be literally pre-sold by the time you ask for the sale.
• 	How is this possible? There are certain mental triggers that cause people to act: authority,
reciprocity, community, social proof, scarcity, etc.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

TRANSCRIPT:
Now we get to the absolute core strategy inside Product Launch Formula. It's
what I call the Sideways Sales LetterTM.
If you’re brand new to Product Launch Formula you might be wondering,
“What does that mean? That sounds pretty weird.”
There are a lot of different paths through these strategies and tactics, but the
one common theme, the one common path for the most successful PLF Owners, for
the people who really take this and put it to use, is the Sideways Sales Letter. That’s
the core strategy.
It’s the opposite of the old style sales letter.
Most of us have been on an old school sales letter. None of us ever go to a
sales letter intentionally, we end up on a sales letter. The web page will load, and
then when it loads, there's this scroll bar off to the side.
The scroll bar is really big when the page first starts loading, or if you have a
slow internet connection like I do. But then that sales letter starts loading up... and it
loads 10 pages or 20 pages, maybe 30 or 40 pages... and that little scroll bar gets
really small as all those pages load, and you end up with this huge 30 page sales
letter. They're really long.
The thing is, people don’t tend to read those long sales letters. What they do is
they grab that scroll bar and they scroll down to the bottom to check what the price is
and then they decide whether they want to scroll back up and maybe read your
headline. Maybe they’ll read a few bullet points, and they’ll come back down to the
offer. That’s what generally happens in the long form sales letter, the old school sales
letter.
There is a time and a place for a long form sales letter. I’m not saying it’s evil,
or you can’t do it, or that it absolutely doesn’t work. There is a time and a place for it.
This is sort of the old school marketing.
The Sideways Sales Letter is where we take this long vertical
sales letter - maybe it’s 10 pages... 20 pages... or 30 pages - and we turn
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

it sideways and we make it a 10 day sequence. That’s the core of Product
Launch Formula.
It's different from one sales message, when you send an email
and you send people to your sales page, your sales video or your sales
letter, and you hope that they buy. That’s what I call hope marketing.
This is the opposite of hope marketing.
Hope is a fantastic word. If you’re shipwrecked and you’re just drifting out on
the sea and you’re hoping someone comes to rescue you. In that kind of situation,
hope can literally keep you alive. But in business, you don’t want to depend on hope.
You want to engineer your success.
When you just email and send someone to a one off sales letter or a sales
video, and you’re hoping they buy, that doesn’t work. This is the Sideways Sales
Letter, the new style that converts now.
This is my depiction of a sideways letter.
These three boxes are your prelaunch, what we call our PLC – Prelaunch
Content. This is a classic internal kind of launch, but conceptually this will work for
all your launches. What you’re doing is you’re putting out content in each one of
those.
This is your first piece of PLC, your second piece of PLC, and your third piece
of PLC. Typically in your Prelaunch Content you’re doing a lot of the same things that
you would do in a sales letter, but you’re spreading it out over time.
What you’re doing is you’re building a relationship, you’re delivering real
value, you’re delivering really great content as you move through this.
The Sideways Sales Letter allows you to do everything you do down in here,
but you get people to pay attention and you’re building rapport as you go along. It
gives you a chance to hit the mental triggers. We’ll be getting to the triggers a little
later in this module.
These mental triggers are the things that cause people to act. They’re things
like authority and reciprocity, and social proof, and scarcity, and community.
In these prelaunch videos, they give you the opportunity to
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

TRANSCRIPT
MODULE ONE – The Sideways Sales Letter™
6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	I 	www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
deploy those mental triggers and to build a relationship.
One of the things they really do is they really help you with the good old B.S.
detector. Basically, think about when you walk onto a used car lot and the salesman
is coming out to walk towards you. Generally, your B.S. detector is going off the
chart, you don’t necessarily believe him and you’re up in the red zone. You know he’s
coming to sell you, you don’t have a lot of faith that you can trust him, so you’re just
not going to believe anything he says. Everything he says, you’re going to take it with
a grain of salt. When someone comes to your old style sales letter, what happens is...
as soon as they see this page load, as soon as they see this headline and see this huge
page load... their B.S. detector goes into the red zone and they’re not going to believe
what you have to say.
They’re going to be very skeptical.
What happens when you come out with Prelaunch Content, and you deliver
some really great value, and then three days later you release another video that
teaches, trains, and gives them real value?
Then what you do is here we’ve got the green zone. The green zone is like
when your mom is telling you, “Wow, I just ate at a really great restaurant. You
should check it out.” Your mom is telling you that, so you’re probably going to believe
her. You don’t think she has an ulterior motive; you really trust her.
Let’s say there’s sort of three zones here. It’s green, black, and red. Here is
mom telling you about a restaurant or whatever, something innocuous. You
completely believe over here. Over here you don’t believe the salesman so much.
They come to your long form sales letter, they’re in the red zone immediately.
But over here... what happens is when you start delivering great content here, and
giving them real value in their lives, and you start hitting these mental triggers... then
you go from being in the red zone and you move over. You might not go over into the
green zone, like they’d believe a mom, but they believe you and they trust you a lot
more and they start to like you.
The big thing about this sideways sales letter is it basically moves people from
skepticism to trust before you ever get to that sale. It's a sequence of actions.
As we go through Product Launch Formula, we’re going to walk through all
kinds of sequences, the timing of this, what to do in each one of these, what to say in

each one of these pieces of Prelaunch Content.
In an Internal Launch, this might be your day one. This is your timeline down
here. Day four. Day seven. And then here you open cart, and this is your sales video
or your sales letter. We might release that on day 10. Again, I’m going to go through
all of this in detail, walk you through all of this as we go through Product Launch
Formula. Different types of launches have different timing, that might just be a
typical Internal Launch.
Your first piece of Prelaunch Content is great for authority.
Generally, if you’re giving out great Prelaunch Content, you’re teaching and that
automatically establishes you as an authority. It’s just an automatic thing. When
you’re publishing, whether it’s publishing online or publishing in any form, as a
publisher people automatically look at you as an authority. When you can just sit
there and give out good content for free, you are seen as an authority.
Another thing, you’ll start to build up reciprocity here. That is if
you give someone something, they will feel some kind of debt to give you something
back. Just by giving them great free content, you’re starting to build this reciprocity.
That will come in handy down the road.
I’m going to go through all these triggers and exactly how they work, how you
use them, and what they mean later in this module and as we go through the course.
I’m just being very conceptual here with the Sideways Sales Letter.
In that first video, you’ll typically also get some social proof.
Social proof is this idea if we see other people doing something, then we think it
might be a good thing. Typically, you’ll get comments or you’ll ask for comments on
that video or that piece of Prelaunch Content. It will be on a blog and people can see
those comments. That’s automatic social proof.
This first piece we call the opportunity.
That’s where you’re teaching people that there’s an opportunity for change.
Real big picture, one of the greatest things I think you can do for someone is show
them that they have an opportunity to change.
Show them they can have an opportunity to lower their golf score. Or they
have an opportunity to make more money as a massage therapist. Or they have an
opportunity to have a better relationship or to attract the love of their life. Or the
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

opportunity to play guitar. You’re showing them that they can change their life. That
they can get better at something, get more of something, reduce their pain.
This first piece is generally about the opportunity to get these
great results, to get this transformation.
The second one is what we call the transformation video.
This is where you start to make it real. Often you’ll have a case study or you’ll
do a lot more heavy-duty teaching in the second video. That way you’re basically
getting more authority, reciprocity, and social proof. There’s this idea of likability
where people start to like you more.
Often we have a lot of community that starts to build up. The
community can actually build up in the comments. Just people helping each other or
answering each other, building on each other’s comments. You can really start to
build some community in that second piece.
This third one is the ownership experience.
In that third one, we’re really starting to give them the feel of what it will be
like to own your product. In this one, we’re hitting authority and reciprocity again,
but we start to build in social proof really big. And scarcity really comes into
play in this one.
What you do is over these seven days, or five days, or 10 days,
you really start to build real trust.
They trust you, they like you, and generally they want to buy from you.
By the time you get to your sales letter or your sales video, you still do the
same kind of things you do in this long form sales letter, but by the time you get here
they are pre-sold.
That is really what the Sideways Sales Letter is all about is, by the time you
ask them to buy from you, they are pre-sold... they’re ready to buy... they can’t wait to
get out their credit card and give you their money.
So we have lots of different ways to deploy the Sideways Sales Letter. It’s not
always videos. It can be PDFs, it could be software, it could be blog posts, it can all be
